FIRST.description: Qualities every unit test in this plan must hold.
FIRST.Fast: Tests run in milliseconds — no real network. HTTP behaviour is exercised with httptest.Server on localhost.
FIRST.Independent: No shared state or ordering between tests. Each test builds its own RequestSpec, server, and channels.
FIRST.Repeatable: Deterministic across machines and runs. No reliance on wall-clock thresholds for assertions on correctness; timing-sensitive cases use generous, stable bounds.
FIRST.SelfValidating: Each test asserts a boolean pass/fail with no manual inspection of output.
FIRST.Timely: Tests are written alongside (or before) the feature in the same commit, per the build plan.

BOC.description: Boundary / Off-by-one / Corner — the case-selection heuristic. Every testable feature enumerates: a Boundary case (edges of valid input), an Off-by-one case (n-1, n, n+1 around limits and index math), and a Corner case (empty, zero, error, concurrent, or otherwise degenerate input).
BOC.B: Boundary — smallest/largest valid input, exact limits.
BOC.O: Off-by-one — the classic index and count errors (len-1 vs len, inclusive vs exclusive).
BOC.C: Corner — empty/nil/zero, error paths, concurrency, and unusual-but-legal combinations.

testCases.title: Defaults applied when optional flags omitted
testCases.given: Only --url is supplied.
testCases.when: Flags are parsed into a RequestSpec.
testCases.then: concurrency=10, requests=100, method=GET, timeout=10s.

testCases.title: Missing --url is rejected
testCases.given: No --url flag.
testCases.then: A non-nil error is returned and no run starts.

testCases.title: Repeatable --header flags accumulate
testCases.given: --header passed twice (e.g. one then two headers).
testCases.then: RequestSpec.Headers contains exactly the N headers given — not N-1, not N+1.

testCases.title: Worker emits one result per job and closes body
testCases.given: A single job and an httptest.Server returning 200.
testCases.then: Exactly one Result with StatusCode 200 and nil Err is sent; the response body is closed.

testCases.title: Worker exits when jobs channel closed empty
testCases.given: A jobs channel closed before any job is sent.
testCases.then: worker() returns immediately, calls wg.Done(), sends zero results.

testCases.title: Worker reports transport error as Result.Err
testCases.given: A target URL whose server is closed/unreachable.
testCases.then: A Result with non-nil Err and zero StatusCode is sent; the loop continues.

testCases.title: Every job produces exactly one collected result
testCases.given: total=1000, concurrency=50, httptest.Server returning 200.
testCases.then: Summary.Total == 1000.

testCases.title: Single-worker, single-request path
testCases.given: concurrency=1, total=1.
testCases.then: Exactly one result is collected and both channels close — proves the n=1 edge of the fan-out loop.

testCases.title: No goroutine leak after run returns
testCases.given: A completed run under the race detector.
testCases.when: run() returns and goroutines are sampled.
testCases.then: Worker count returns to baseline; no -race report. (go test -race)

testCases.title: Errors counted separately from latencies
testCases.given: A results channel pre-loaded with 2 errors and 3 successes.
testCases.then: Total=5, Errors=2, len(Latencies)=3; error results contribute no latency.

testCases.title: Collect terminates on closed channel
testCases.given: A results channel that is closed after loading items.
testCases.then: collect() returns without blocking once the channel is drained.

testCases.title: Empty latency slice returns zero
testCases.given: An empty sorted []time.Duration.
testCases.then: Returns 0 and does not panic.

testCases.title: p50/p95/p99 select correct index (table-driven)
testCases.given: A known sorted slice of 100 durations (1ms..100ms).
testCases.then: Returns the values at indices floor(99*p) — table-driven []struct{ input, p, want }.

testCases.title: Off-by-one: index uses len-1 not len
testCases.given: A sorted slice of length n.
testCases.then: Returns sorted[n-1] (the max), never indexing sorted[n] / out of range.

testCases.title: Single-element slice
testCases.given: A one-element sorted slice.
testCases.when: percentile is evaluated at any p in [0,1].
testCases.then: Always returns that single element.

testCases.title: Throughput is requests over elapsed
testCases.given: A Summary of 1000 successes over a known 0.8s elapsed.
testCases.then: Throughput == 1250 req/s within rounding tolerance.

testCases.title: Error breakdown rendered per kind
testCases.given: ErrorKinds = {timeout:2, refused:1}.
testCases.then: Output lists both kinds with counts and a total of 3 errors.

testCases.title: Atomic counter reaches total under race detector
testCases.given: concurrency=50, total=1000, run with -race.
testCases.when: All workers increment the shared int64.
testCases.then: Final atomic.LoadInt64 == 1000 with no race report.

testCases.title: Concurrent increments lose no updates
testCases.given: Many goroutines each adding 1 a fixed number of times.
testCases.then: Counter equals goroutines × iterations exactly — proving lock-free correctness.

testCases.title: progressBar fill is proportional
testCases.given: A fixed bar width W.
testCases.when: progressBar(done, total) is called for done in {0, total/2, total-1, total}.
testCases.then: Filled cells equal floor(W*done/total); the total-1 vs total step differs by the off-by-one boundary.

testCases.title: progressBar clamps at 100%
testCases.given: done == total (and done > total guard).
testCases.then: Bar is fully filled and never exceeds width W.

testCases.title: DeadlineExceeded classified as timeout
testCases.given: An error wrapping context.DeadlineExceeded.
testCases.then: Returns "timeout" via errors.Is.

testCases.title: net.Error with Timeout() true classified as timeout
testCases.given: A fake net.Error whose Timeout() returns true.
testCases.then: Returns "timeout" via errors.As.

testCases.title: Connection refused classified as refused
testCases.given: An error whose message contains "connection refused".
testCases.then: Returns "refused".

testCases.title: Unknown error falls through to other
testCases.given: An arbitrary error matching no rule.
testCases.then: Returns "other".

testCases.title: Slow endpoint exceeding timeout errors out
testCases.given: httptest.Server that sleeps longer than --timeout.
testCases.when: A request runs with that timeout.
testCases.then: Result.Err is non-nil and classifies as "timeout".

testCases.title: Context cancelled on run return
testCases.given: A run that completes normally.
testCases.then: The cancel func has fired (defer cancel) and ctx.Done() is closed.

testCases.title: Duration mode stops feeding when window elapses
testCases.given: --duration set to a short window against a fast server.
testCases.when: run() feeds jobs until the duration context fires.
testCases.then: jobs is closed promptly after the window and run() returns; Summary.Total > 0.

testCases.title: Duration mode ignores --requests count
testCases.given: Both --duration and a small --requests are provided.
testCases.then: The run is bounded by time, not by the request count.

testCases.title: MaxIdleConnsPerHost equals concurrency
testCases.given: concurrency=50.
testCases.then: Transport.MaxIdleConnsPerHost == 50.

testCases.title: README documents every flag
testCases.given: The set of CLI flags from F-1.1.
testCases.when: The README flag reference is reviewed (manually or via a doc-lint).
testCases.then: Each flag appears with its default; the fan-out/fan-in diagram is present.

testCases.title: go install produces a runnable binary
testCases.given: A clean GOPATH/bin.
testCases.then: A `blaze` binary exists and `blaze --help` exits zero.
